title: AGENTS.md
text: package thin R/savvy surface upstream maximecb/bebelm, plus small dedicated pure-Rust EmbeddingGemma encoder. Keep focused: native model loading, tokenization, retrieval embeddings, contextual-state extraction, generation, persistent agents, R tool calls, async jobs, backend diagnostics.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Rust backend owns model integration. R expose typed calls printers, second agent inference framework. Keep EmbeddingGemma stateless separate BebelModel: Rust crate owns GGUF validation, SentencePiece tokenization, bidirectional attention, mean pooling, learned projections, Matryoshka truncation. Validate pinned external oracle without linking oracle package. BebelModel instances load GGUF weights . BebelAgent instances keep independent transcript/decode state share model weights Rust backend. Async APIs return BebelAsyncJob objects. Rust workers may enqueue plain generation events; R drains main thread bebel_async_events(). R tool execution also belongs R main thread. Tools BebelToolSpec S7 objects. Keep validation S7 properties validators instead adding checker helper functions. JSON handling uses imported yyjsonr. Preserve webR loadability. Unsupported native inference fail model loading useful error, package load. strict SIMD dispatch. Portable/scalar artifacts must compiled unguarded native CPU assumptions.

title: Late-interaction retrieval with ColBERT
text: native profile implements release contract: [Q] [D] prefixes, 32-position query (including learned PAD expansion positions), 512-position document, published document punctuation filtering, learned dense projection, per-token L2 normalization. model semantics, caller options.

title: Late-interaction retrieval with ColBERT
text: colbert_rank() small exact scorer re-ranking candidate documents. deliberately claim ANN compressed late-interaction index; production corpus indexed system preserves model preprocessing contract.
code: colbert_rank(   model,   "capital of Mali",   c(     mali = "Bamako is the capital and largest city of Mali.",     italy = "Rome is the capital city of Italy.",     desert = "The Sahara is a desert in northern Africa."   ) ) ## <ColBERT MaxSim ranking> ##     mali    italy   desert  ## 30.60933 29.99548 29.56865

title: Retrieval embeddings with EmbeddingGemma
text: BebelModel EmbeddingGemmaModel deliberately different APIs. LFM2.5-8B-A1B causal generator. EmbeddingGemma retrieval-trained, bidirectional text encoder whose final vectors intended cosine similarity, retrieval, clustering, related representation tasks. package’s EmbeddingGemma path implemented Rust. reads GGUF tokenizer metadata directly, runs: BOS/content/EOS SentencePiece tokenization; 24 bidirectional Gemma layers, using five symmetric-window layers followed one global-attention layer; final RMSNorm attention-mask mean pooling; learned 768--3072 3072--768 projections; optional Matryoshka truncation followed L2 normalization. llama.cpp, PyTorch, ONNX Runtime, SentencePiece C++ library linked package. Oracle tests use pinned ggml-org Q8_0 artifact revision 0f741b5a6585bd53aeb15cd1372c56f2a0f65e12 (SHA-256 b5ce9d77a3fc4b3b39ccb5643c36777911cc4eb46a66962eadfa3f5f60490d63).

title: Retrieval embeddings with EmbeddingGemma
text: EmbeddingGemma trained asymmetric retrieval prompts. Use dedicated helpers rather embedding unformatted query document strings. Character-vector inputs packed bounded encoder batches reuse model matrix reads. Position attention boundaries reset every element, batched result numerically equivalent encoding string separately. Document titles optional. NULL applies model’s literal title: none prompt; title scalar recycled input vector.
code: query <- embeddinggemma_embed_query(model, "Which city is the capital of Mali?") passages <- c(   mali = "Bamako is the capital and largest city of Mali.",   italy = "Rome is the capital city of Italy.",   desert = "The Sahara is a desert in northern Africa." ) documents <- embeddinggemma_embed_document(model, passages)  scores <- drop(documents %*% as.numeric(query)) sort(scores, decreasing = TRUE) ##      mali     italy    desert  ## 0.6511533 0.2656509 0.1596326 with_titles <- embeddinggemma_embed_document(   model,   passages[c("mali", "italy")],   title = c("Mali", "Italy") ) with_titles ## <EmbeddingGemma embeddings> ##   shape: 2 x 768 ##   task: retrieval_document ##   normalized: yes

title: Retrieval embeddings with EmbeddingGemma
text: generic function requires explicit task silently mix retrieval-document, retrieval-query, semantic-similarity prompts. Supported dimensions 768, 512, 256, 128. Lower-dimensional outputs leading dimensions learned 768-vector re-normalized. Index query vectors must always use task contract, model artifact, dimension, normalization setting.
code: similarity_embeddings <- embeddinggemma_embed(   model,   c("A pleasant day", "Beautiful weather"),   task = "semantic_similarity",   dimensions = 256L )  similarity_embeddings %*% t(similarity_embeddings) ##           [,1]      [,2] ## [1,] 1.0000000 0.8796266 ## [2,] 0.8796266 1.0000000 attr(similarity_embeddings, "embedding_info") ## $model ## [1] "EmbeddingGemma-300M" ##  ## $architecture ## [1] "gemma-embedding" ##  ## $source_task ## [1] "contrastive text representation learning" ##  ## $task ## [1] "semantic_similarity" ##  ## $prompt_applied ## [1] TRUE ##  ## $pooling ## [1] "attention-mask mean" ##  ## $projection ## [1]  768 3072  768 ##  ## $dimensions ## [1] 256 ##  ## $l2_normalized ## [1] TRUE ##  ## $retrieval_trained ## [1] TRUE ##  ## $context_length ## [1] 2048 ##  ## $token_count ## [1] 12 11 ##  ## $truncated ## [1] FALSE FALSE

what: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
title: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
text: Rbebelm provides local pure-Rust CPU inference three complementary GGUF models: Liquid AI LFM2.5-8B-A1B maximecb/bebelm, Google’s retrieval-trained EmbeddingGemma-300M LiquidAI’s LFM2.5-ColBERT-350M dedicated native encoders. package provides model loading, tokenizer access, semantic text embeddings, trained late-interaction token vectors MaxSim scoring, bounded generation, persistent agents, BebeLM tool-call parsing, R tool dispatch, stream events, Rust-thread async jobs. ship weights. Set BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE, EMBEDDING_GEMMA_WEIGHTS_FILE, COLBERT_WEIGHTS_FILE local GGUF paths pass paths directly. EmbeddingGemma weights governed Gemma Terms Use.

title: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
text: Source installs require Cargo/rustc GNU make. Native builds compile scalar SIMD backend libraries supported; runtime dispatcher selects best installed backend current CPU.

title: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
text: GGUF memory-mapped read-upstream BebeLM. Multiple agents created one BebelModel share -process Arc<Model>. Async jobs created model also share weights keeping independent caches. Separate processes load GGUF can share physical pages operating-system page cache. Threading set model loaded. num_threads initializes process-global Rayon pool ; generation retrieval calls use pool. Async calls add one Rust worker per job can execute concurrently, total CPU demand controlled model-load thread count many jobs caller launches.
code: library(Rbebelm)  model <- bebel_model_load(weights_file, num_threads = bebel_threads) model #> <BebelModel> #>   path: /root/bebelm/LFM2.5-8B-A1B-Q4_K_M.gguf #>   backend: avx2 rbebelm_backend_features() #> <Rbebelm backend features> #>   backend: avx2 #>   target: x86_64-linux #>   Rust crate: rbebelm_backend 0.1.0 #>   native SIMD feature: yes #>   compiled features: #>     AVX2: yes #>     AVX-512F: no #>     NEON: no #>     ARM dotprod: no #>     wasm simd128: no #>   model storage: read-only GGUF mmap; repeated loads of the same file share physical pages through the OS page cache

title: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
code: ids <- bebel_tokenize(model, "Bamako", add_bos = FALSE) ids #> [1]   42  330 6261 bebel_detokenize(model, ids) #> [1] "Bamako"

title: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
text: EmbeddingGemma separate stateless model handle. Rust implementation loads gemma-embedding GGUF directly runs bidirectional encoder, mean pooling, learned dense projections, L2 normalization without linking llama.cpp, PyTorch, ONNX Runtime, SentencePiece C++ library. query document helpers apply different prompts distinction part model’s training contract. Character vectors use bounded packed encoder batches retaining independent positions attention boundaries. embeddinggemma_embed() requires explicit task uses. Set dimensions 512, 256, 128 Matryoshka truncation re-normalization.
code: embedding_model <- embeddinggemma_model_load(   embedding_weights_file,   num_threads = bebel_threads )  query_embedding <- embeddinggemma_embed_query(   embedding_model,   "capital of Mali" ) document_embeddings <- embeddinggemma_embed_document(   embedding_model,   c(     mali = "Bamako is the capital and largest city of Mali.",     italy = "Rome is the capital city of Italy.",     desert = "The Sahara is a desert in northern Africa."   ) )  sort(drop(document_embeddings %*% as.numeric(query_embedding)), decreasing = TRUE) #>      mali     italy    desert #> 0.6257073 0.2532762 0.1605835

title: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
text: LFM2.5-ColBERT-350M separate, retrieval-trained bidirectional encoder. produces 128-dimensional L2-normalized vectors individual query document tokens, scores query ColBERT MaxSim rather reducing text single pooled vector. encoder owns model’s published [Q] [D] prefixes, 32-query 512-document limits, punctuation filtering, token-vector L2 normalization, MaxSim contract. colbert_rank() exact candidate scoring; use dedicated late-interaction index large corpora. Set COLBERT_WEIGHTS_FILE local LFM2.5-ColBERT-350M GGUF execute example rendering README.
code: colbert_model <- colbert_model_load(   colbert_weights_file,   num_threads = bebel_threads )  ranking <- colbert_rank(   colbert_model,   "capital of Mali",   c(     mali = "Bamako is the capital and largest city of Mali.",     italy = "Rome is the capital city of Italy.",     desert = "The Sahara is a desert in northern Africa."   ) ) ranking #> <ColBERT MaxSim ranking> #>     mali    italy   desert #> 30.60933 29.99548 29.56865  query_vectors <- colbert_encode_query(colbert_model, "capital of Mali") colbert_embeddings_info(query_vectors) #> $kind #> [1] "query" #> #> $tokens #> [1] 32 #> #> $dimensions #> [1] 128

code: lookup_capital <- bebel_tool(   "lookup_capital",   function(args, context, call) {     context$calls <- c(context$calls, paste(call$name, args$country))     c(Mali = "Bamako", Italy = "Rome", France = "Paris")[[args$country]]   },   description = "Return a capital city.",   schema = list(     properties = list(country = list(type = "string")),     required = list("country")   ) )  lookup_capital #> <BebelToolSpec> lookup_capital #>   Return a capital city. bebel_tool_schema_json(lookup_capital) #> [1] "{\"name\":\"lookup_capital\",\"description\":\"Return a capital city.\",\"parameters\":{\"properties\":{\"country\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"country\"],\"type\":\"object\"}}"  ctx <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) ctx$calls <- character() call <- bebel_parse_tool_call('[lookup_capital(country="Mali")]') Rbebelm:::invoke_bebel_tool(lookup_capital, call, ctx) #> [1] "Bamako" ctx$calls #> [1] "lookup_capital Mali"

title: Native BebeLM, EmbeddingGemma, and ColBERT CPU Backends for R
text: Async jobs use aio-style surface: submit work, monitor queued events R thread, collect completed Turn. bebel_async_wait() blocking monitor used model-level sync helpers, stream callbacks always run R thread. Several jobs can share one loaded model; weights stay shared execution can overlap. Agent async jobs run cloned agent snapshot: original agent mutated.
code: job_a <- bebel_generate_async(   model,   "The capital of Italy is",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 8,   max_think = 0 )  job_b_agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 8, max_think = 0) bebel_append(job_b_agent, "The capital of Mali is") job_b <- bebel_agent_generate_async(job_b_agent)  event_types_a <- character() async_a <- bebel_async_wait(   job_a,   on_event = function(event) event_types_a <<- c(event_types_a, event$type) ) async_b <- bebel_async_collect(job_b, wait = TRUE)  unique(event_types_a) #> [1] "start"      "text_start" "text_delta" "text_end"   "done" async_a #> <BebeLM generation result> #>   stop: max_new #>   tokens: 8 generated; 6 prompt #>   prefill: 23.2 tok/s #>   decode: 21.22 tok/s #>   text: #>  Rome. city of... ... ... ... async_b #> <BebeLM generation result> #>   stop: max_new #>   tokens: 8 generated; 6 prompt #>   prefill: 22.8 tok/s #>   decode: 21.07 tok/s #>   text: #>  the city of Bamako. city of bebel_agent_info(job_b_agent)[c("history_tokens", "processed_tokens")] #> $history_tokens #> [1] 6 #> #> $processed_tokens #> [1] 0

title: Rbebelm 0.3.6-0.1.0
text: Replaced experimental causal contextual-state retrieval surface native, retrieval-trained LFM2.5-ColBERT-350M GGUF profile. New colbert_*() APIs published query/document prefixes, non-causal encoder, 128-dimensional L2-normalized token projection, punctuation filtering, MaxSim scoring contract. bebel_pooled_states() bebel_token_states() removed: causal generator states late-interaction retriever. Established vendored BebeLM Rust backend closed, validated CPU profile registry rather generic GGUF loader. New model families add tensor contract execution profile; BebeLM generation continues support LFM2.5-8B-A1B profile. Added dedicated pure-Rust gemma-embedding backend 300M EmbeddingGemma GGUF architecture, including SentencePiece tokenizer, bidirectional global/symmetric-window attention schedule, mean pooling, learned dense projections, task-specific query/document prompts, L2 normalization, 768/512/256/128-dimensional Matryoshka outputs. Batches use bounded sequence packing without cross-sequence attention parallel GeGLU evaluation. New embeddinggemma_*() APIs keep retrieval-trained dense embeddings separate BebeLM generation. Fixed AArch64 scalar NEON artifacts avoid dot-product instructions; dot-product artifact alone compiled CPU feature. Added AArch64 cross-build check runtime-dispatch variants. Exposed existing "dotprod" backend explicit R selection. Refocused package native BebeLM model loading, tokenization, retrieval-trained encoders, generation, persistent agents, R tool calls, async jobs. Removed R-side agent framework, interactive frontend, session tree, extension layer, provider registry, fuzzy-file-search code. Added S7 classes property validators model options, agent options, generation options, tool specs, native pointer refs. Added async model agent generation Rust worker threads. Jobs represented BebelAsyncJob objects collected R bebel_async_collect(). Removed model execution mutex async jobs can run concurrently sharing immutable memory-mapped weights keeping independent caches. Surfaced shared-weight model design: agents transcript/decode state sharing loaded GGUF weights Rust backend. Updated README, vignettes, pkgdown metadata, tinytests, webR package-load check around real BebeLM runs lean public API. Hardened native SIMD dispatch: scalar builds use baseline target optimized dylibs selected runtime CPU checks.
